name: Bill's Pot Roast
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large Dutch oven, heat Worcestershire sauce, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
2. Add some margarine. When the oil is hot, sear the roast quickly on all sides.
3. Add beef broth and onion soup, plus seasonings to taste.
4. Reduce heat to medium and bring to a brisk simmer (not a rolling boil).
5. Cook covered for 2 hours, or until tender.
6. Peel and cut up vegetables into similar-sized slices. Add them to the roast.
7. When potatoes are fork-tender, remove cloves and serve.
8. If the liquid level drops below half of what you started with, add water.
9. To reheat: Prepare fresh vegetables. Cover the pot and bring to a simmer.
10. When warm, take the meat out and place it on top of the vegetables (so they just steam);
cook for 45 minutes. You may want to add soup instead of water.
